message:
docs(readme): fix df.transform() example to match real signatures (br-7135r / fd90.107)

fd90.107: line 649 in 'Multiple ways to apply custom logic' showed:

  let normed = df.transform(\"zscore\")?;

Two compounding errors:

1. crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:19590 declares DataFrame::transform as
   pub fn transform<F>(&self, func: F) -> Result<Self, FrameError>
   where F: Fn(&Scalar) -> Scalar
   It takes a closure, NOT a string. Passing \"zscore\" wouldn't compile.

2. The string-based variant only exists on DataFrameGroupBy (matches
   df.groupby(col).transform(func_name) semantics) and apply_agg_func
   only supports: sum, mean, count, min, max, std, var, median, first,
   last, nunique, prod. \"zscore\" is NOT supported and would error
   'unsupported groupby aggregation: zscore'.

Fix: replace with two valid examples:
  - DataFrame::transform with a per-element closure showing the actual
    Fn(&Scalar) -> Scalar contract
  - DataFrameGroupBy::transform(\"mean\") demonstrating the per-group
    reduction broadcast semantics with a name that's actually accepted

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
